title: Poisson Surface Reconstruction
text: demo uses hand.vtp VESPA test suite — dense triangulated surface mesh human hand (61 082 vertices, 122 160 triangles). deliberately discard face connectivity retain vertex positions, turning mesh raw, unstructured point cloud. lets us run full reconstruction pipeline: normal estimation PCA, Poisson reconstruction. original mesh serve ground-truth visual comparison end. strip face connectivity obtain raw, unstructured point cloud:
code: hand_mesh <- read_vtp(hand_tmp) print(hand_mesh) #> <mesh3d> 61082 vertices 122160 faces hand_pts <- hand_mesh hand_pts$it <- matrix(integer(0), 3L, 0L)

title: Poisson Surface Reconstruction
text: poisson_reconstruction() requires oriented normals. point cloud none (case , since stripped mesh connectivity per-vertex normal information), use pca_estimate_normals(): fits local plane n_neighbors nearest neighbours derives normal plane. Setting orient = TRUE propagates consistent orientation across cloud via minimum spanning tree normal graph.
code: hand_pts <- pca_estimate_normals(hand_pts, n_neighbors = 18L, orient = TRUE)

title: Poisson Surface Reconstruction
text: three key parameters control density quality output mesh:
code: hand_recon <- poisson_reconstruction(   hand_pts,   min_angle = 20,   max_size  = 2,   distance  = 0.375 ) print(hand_recon) #> <mesh3d> 72970 vertices 23772 faces

title: Poisson Surface Reconstruction
text: Poisson method tends produce smooth, slightly blurred reconstruction implicit solve acts low-pass filter. Tightening distance max_size recovers finer surface details cost higher face count.
code: summary(hand_mesh) #>  #> ── mesh3d #> Vertices: 61082 #> Faces: 122160 #> X range: [-0.96596, 0.89204] #> Y range: [-0.64539, 0.70538] #> Z range: [-0.51493, 0.34991] #> Diagonal: 2.4545 #> Normals: no #>  summary(hand_recon) #>  #> ── mesh3d #> Vertices: 72970 #> Faces: 23772 #> X range: [-0.96596, 0.89204] #> Y range: [-0.64539, 0.70538] #> Z range: [-0.51493, 0.34991] #> Diagonal: 2.4545 #> Normals: no rgl::mfrow3d(1, 2) rgl::points3d(t(hand_pts$vb[1:3, ]), size = 1, col = "steelblue") rgl::title3d("Input point cloud") rgl::next3d() rgl::shade3d(hand_recon, col = "tomato") rgl::title3d("Poisson reconstruction")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Christophe Regouby. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Regouby C (2026). vespa: R Bindings Vespa VTK/CGAL Mesh Processing Library.. R package version 0.4.0, https://cregouby.github.io/vespa/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {vespa: R Bindings for the Vespa VTK/CGAL Mesh Processing Library.},   author = {Christophe Regouby},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.4.0},   url = {https://cregouby.github.io/vespa/}, }

what: R Bindings for the Vespa VTK/CGAL Mesh Processing Library.
title: R Bindings for the Vespa VTK/CGAL Mesh Processing Library.
text: vespa R package providing bindings VESPA C++/VTK/CGAL mesh processing library. exposes CGAL geometry surface processing algorithms R functions operating mesh3d objects (package rgl).  Isotropic remeshing surface mesh  Surface reconstruction point cloud (input cloud / reconstructed mesh)  -Rigid--Possible mesh deformation (original / deformed)
code: library(vespa) mesh   <- read_stl(system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa")) remesh <- isotropic_remeshing(mesh, target_length = 0.1, n_iterations = 3L) rgl::shade3d(remesh, col = "steelblue") library(vespa) pts   <- read_points_xyz(system.file("testdata", "test_points.xyz",                                      package = "vespa")) pts   <- pca_estimate_normals(pts, n_neighbors = 18L, orient = TRUE) recon <- poisson_reconstruction(pts) rgl::shade3d(recon, col = "tomato") library(vespa) mesh <- read_stl(system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa"))  n        <- ncol(mesh$vb) fixed    <- which(mesh$vb[3, ] < -0.25)          # bottom ring — fixed handles moved    <- which(mesh$vb[3, ] >  0.25)           # top ring    — pulled up target   <- mesh$vb[1:3, moved, drop = FALSE] target[3, ] <- target[3, ] + 0.4                  # translate +0.4 along Z  deformed <- mesh_deformation(mesh,                              control_ids   = c(fixed, moved),                              target_coords = cbind(mesh$vb[1:3, fixed], target),                              roi_ids       = seq_len(n)) rgl::shade3d(deformed, col = "goldenrod")

title: R Bindings for the Vespa VTK/CGAL Mesh Processing Library.
text: VESPA C++ library requires VTK >= 9.0 CGAL >= 5.3. Ceres. must install software package manager.

title: R Bindings for the Vespa VTK/CGAL Mesh Processing Library.
text: Installation VESPA C++ library shall follow Vespa Installation Documentation. rely VESPA installed path CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX environemnt variable

title: R Bindings for the Vespa VTK/CGAL Mesh Processing Library.
text: soon VESPA installation path environemnt variable CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX, can provide installation script : terminal
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install.github("cregouby/vespa", configure.vars = "VESPA_ROOT=$CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX") cd vespa export VESPA_ROOT=$CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX R CMD INSTALL .

what: R package installation step by step (development)
title: R Bindings for the Vespa VTK/CGAL Mesh Processing Library.
text: Run configure script building src/Makevars generated: Override auto-detected paths needed:
code: ./configure VESPA_DIR=/usr/local/bin/vespa \ VTK_DIR=/usr/local/lib/cmake/vtk-9.5 \ CGAL_INC=/opt/homebrew/include \ ./configure

title: R Bindings for the Vespa VTK/CGAL Mesh Processing Library.
text: filters expect triangulated, watertight, 2-manifold surface represented mesh3d object. Use mesh_check() diagnose topological issues alpha_wrapping() repair mesh watertight 2-manifold.

title: R Bindings for the Vespa VTK/CGAL Mesh Processing Library.
text: package distributed BSD-3 license. links CGAL, binary built retains GPLv3 license unless hold commercial license GeometryFactory.

what: Reconstruct a surface from a point cloud (Advancing Front) — advancing_front_reconstruction
title: Reconstruct a surface from a point cloud (Advancing Front) — advancing_front_reconstruction
text: Reconstructs triangulated surface unoriented point cloud using CGAL's advancing front surface reconstruction algorithm. Unlike Poisson reconstruction, normals required.

title: Reconstruct a surface from a point cloud (Advancing Front) — advancing_front_reconstruction
code: advancing_front_reconstruction(mesh, per = 0, radius_ratio_bound = 5)

title: Reconstruct a surface from a point cloud (Advancing Front) — advancing_front_reconstruction
text: mesh mesh3d object representing point cloud (may empty $face matrix). per Priority function parameter controlling trade-advancing front size point proximity. Default 0 (automatic). radius_ratio_bound Maximum radius ratio generated triangles (quality bound). Default 5.

title: Reconstruct a surface from a point cloud (Advancing Front) — advancing_front_reconstruction
text: mesh3d object reconstructed surface.

title: Reconstruct a surface from a point cloud (Advancing Front) — advancing_front_reconstruction
code: # \donttest{ f     <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa") mesh  <- read_stl(f) cloud <- mesh; cloud$it <- matrix(integer(0), 3L, 0L) result <- advancing_front_reconstruction(cloud) # }

what: Generate a watertight mesh via alpha wrapping — alpha_wrapping
title: Generate a watertight mesh via alpha wrapping — alpha_wrapping
text: Constructs 2-manifold, watertight surface mesh point cloud triangle soup using CGAL's alpha wrapping algorithm.

title: Generate a watertight mesh via alpha wrapping — alpha_wrapping
code: alpha_wrapping(   mesh,   alpha = 5,   offset = 3,   absolute_thresholds = FALSE,   update_attributes = TRUE )

title: Generate a watertight mesh via alpha wrapping — alpha_wrapping
text: mesh mesh3d object (input point cloud triangle soup). alpha Maximum circumradius output faces. Smaller values produce tighter wraps capture finer concavities. Default 5 (interpreted 5% bounding-box diagonal absolute_thresholds = FALSE). offset Mesh dilatation amount. Default 3. absolute_thresholds TRUE, alpha offset units mesh coordinates. FALSE (default), percentages bounding-box diagonal. update_attributes Whether interpolate point cell data input. Default TRUE.

title: Generate a watertight mesh via alpha wrapping — alpha_wrapping
text: mesh3d object representing wrapped surface.

title: Generate a watertight mesh via alpha wrapping — alpha_wrapping
code: # \donttest{ f <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa") mesh <- read_stl(f) wrapped <- alpha_wrapping(mesh, alpha = 0.5, absolute_thresholds = TRUE) # }

what: Boolean operation on two triangular surface meshes — boolean_operation
title: Boolean operation on two triangular surface meshes — boolean_operation
text: Computes union, intersection, difference two closed triangulated surface meshes using CGAL's polygon mesh processing boolean operations. meshes must closed (watertight) free self-intersections.

title: Boolean operation on two triangular surface meshes — boolean_operation
code: boolean_operation(   mesh_a,   mesh_b,   operation = c("difference", "intersection", "union"),   update_attributes = TRUE )

title: Boolean operation on two triangular surface meshes — boolean_operation
text: mesh_a mesh3d object (first operand). mesh_b mesh3d object (second operand). operation One "difference" (default), "intersection", "union". difference mesh_a minus mesh_b. update_attributes Whether interpolate point cell data inputs. Default TRUE.

title: Boolean operation on two triangular surface meshes — boolean_operation
text: mesh3d object representing result boolean operation.

title: Boolean operation on two triangular surface meshes — boolean_operation
code: # \donttest{ f <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa") mesh  <- read_stl(f) mesh2 <- mesh mesh2$vb[1, ] <- mesh2$vb[1, ] + 0.5  # shift copy along X result <- boolean_operation(mesh, mesh2, operation = "union") # }

what: 2D Delaunay triangulation of a planar point set — delaunay2
title: 2D Delaunay triangulation of a planar point set — delaunay2
text: Computes Delaunay triangulation set 2D points using CGAL's constrained Delaunay algorithm. Optional edge constraints force specific edges triangulation.

title: 2D Delaunay triangulation of a planar point set — delaunay2
code: delaunay2(points, constraints = NULL)

title: 2D Delaunay triangulation of a planar point set — delaunay2
text: points numeric matrix 2 columns (x, y), one row per point. constraints optional integer matrix 2 columns 1-based vertex indices, one row per constraint edge. Default NULL (unconstrained Delaunay triangulation).

title: 2D Delaunay triangulation of a planar point set — delaunay2
text: sfc_POLYGON object (sf package) containing one triangle polygon per Delaunay triangle.

title: 2D Delaunay triangulation of a planar point set — delaunay2
code: pts <- matrix(c(0,0, 1,0, 1,1, 0,1, 0.5,0.5), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE) triangles <- delaunay2(pts) length(triangles)  # number of Delaunay triangles #> [1] 4

what: Extract an isosurface from a 3-D scalar field — extract_isosurface
title: Extract an isosurface from a 3-D scalar field — extract_isosurface
text: Generic function extracts isosurface volumetric scalar field. Currently implemented objects class sdf_volume (returned signed_distance_function()).

title: Extract an isosurface from a 3-D scalar field — extract_isosurface
code: extract_isosurface(x, isovalue = 0, ...)  # S3 method for class 'sdf_volume' extract_isosurface(x, isovalue = 0, ...)  # Default S3 method extract_isosurface(x, isovalue = 0, ...)

title: Extract an isosurface from a 3-D scalar field — extract_isosurface
text: x object extract isosurface. isovalue Numeric. iso-level extract surface. ... Additional arguments passed methods.

title: Extract an isosurface from a 3-D scalar field — extract_isosurface
text: mesh3d object (package rgl).

what: Isotropic remeshing of a triangular surface mesh — isotropic_remeshing
title: Isotropic remeshing of a triangular surface mesh — isotropic_remeshing
text: Remeshes triangulated surface edges approach uniform target length, using CGAL's isotropic remeshing algorithm.

title: Isotropic remeshing of a triangular surface mesh — isotropic_remeshing
code: isotropic_remeshing(   mesh,   target_length = -1,   protect_angle = 45,   n_iterations = 1L,   update_attributes = TRUE )

title: Isotropic remeshing of a triangular surface mesh — isotropic_remeshing
text: mesh mesh3d object (rgl package). target_length Target edge length result. value -1 (default) lets Vespa choose automatically (1% bounding box diagonal). protect_angle Feature edges whose dihedral angle exceeds threshold (degrees) preserved. Default 45. n_iterations Number remeshing iterations. Default 1. update_attributes Whether interpolate point cell data arrays input mesh. Default TRUE.

title: Isotropic remeshing of a triangular surface mesh — isotropic_remeshing
text: mesh3d object remeshed surface.

title: Isotropic remeshing of a triangular surface mesh — isotropic_remeshing
code: # \donttest{ f <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa") mesh <- read_stl(f) result <- isotropic_remeshing(mesh, n_iterations = 1L) # }

what: Validate and optionally repair a triangular surface mesh — mesh_check
title: Validate and optionally repair a triangular surface mesh — mesh_check
text: Checks mesh watertightness self-intersections using CGAL's polygon mesh processing algorithms. Issues reported R warnings. attempt_repair = TRUE, Vespa attempts fix detected problems returns repaired mesh.

title: Validate and optionally repair a triangular surface mesh — mesh_check
code: mesh_check(   mesh,   check_watertight = TRUE,   check_intersect = TRUE,   attempt_repair = FALSE )

title: Validate and optionally repair a triangular surface mesh — mesh_check
text: mesh mesh3d object. check_watertight Whether check mesh closed bounds volume. Default TRUE. check_intersect Whether check self-intersections. Default TRUE. attempt_repair TRUE, Vespa attempts fix detected issues (fill holes, re-orient faces, remove self-intersections). Default FALSE.

title: Validate and optionally repair a triangular surface mesh — mesh_check
text: mesh3d object — identical input unless attempt_repair = TRUE repairs applied. R warnings emitted issue detected.

title: Validate and optionally repair a triangular surface mesh — mesh_check
code: f <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa") mesh <- read_stl(f) result <- mesh_check(mesh)

what: Deform a triangular surface mesh — mesh_deformation
title: Deform a triangular surface mesh — mesh_deformation
text: Deforms triangulated surface mesh moving set control vertices specified target positions, propagating deformation across surface using CGAL's -rigid--possible (ARAP) smoothed rotation enhanced ARAP (SRE-ARAP) algorithms.

title: Deform a triangular surface mesh — mesh_deformation
code: mesh_deformation(   mesh,   control_ids,   target_coords,   roi_ids = integer(0),   mode = c("smooth", "sre_arap"),   sre_alpha = 0.02,   n_iterations = 5L,   tolerance = 1e-04 )

title: Deform a triangular surface mesh — mesh_deformation
text: mesh mesh3d object. control_ids Integer vector 1-based vertex indices act handles. target_coords Numeric matrix length(control_ids) rows 3 columns (x, y, z). Target world-space positions handle. roi_ids Integer vector 1-based vertex indices forming region influence. Use integer(0) (default) let filter determine region automatically. mode Deformation algorithm: "smooth" (ARAP, default) "sre_arap" (Smoothed Rotation Enhanced ARAP). sre_alpha Blending weight SRE-ARAP (0 = pure ARAP, 1 = pure shape). Default 0.02. n_iterations Number optimisation iterations. Default 5L. tolerance Convergence tolerance. Default 1e-4.

title: Deform a triangular surface mesh — mesh_deformation
text: mesh3d object deformed surface.

title: Deform a triangular surface mesh — mesh_deformation
code: # \donttest{ f <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa") mesh <- read_stl(f) orig   <- mesh$vb[1:3, 1] target <- matrix(orig + c(0.1, 0, 0), nrow = 1) result <- mesh_deformation(mesh, control_ids = 1L, target_coords = target) # }

what: Smooth a triangular surface mesh — mesh_smoothing
title: Smooth a triangular surface mesh — mesh_smoothing
text: Smooths triangulated surface using CGAL's polygon mesh processing smoothing algorithms.

title: Smooth a triangular surface mesh — mesh_smoothing
code: mesh_smoothing(   mesh,   method = c("tangential", "angle_area"),   n_iterations = 10L,   safety_constraints = FALSE,   update_attributes = TRUE )

title: Smooth a triangular surface mesh — mesh_smoothing
text: mesh mesh3d object. method Smoothing method: "tangential" (default) tangential relaxation, "angle_area" angle area regularisation. n_iterations Number smoothing iterations. Default 10L. safety_constraints TRUE, applies safety constraints limit vertex displacement. Default FALSE. update_attributes Whether interpolate point cell data input. Default TRUE.

title: Smooth a triangular surface mesh — mesh_smoothing
text: mesh3d object smoothed surface.

title: Smooth a triangular surface mesh — mesh_smoothing
code: # \donttest{ f <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa") mesh <- read_stl(f) smooth <- mesh_smoothing(mesh, method = "tangential", n_iterations = 3L) # }

what: Subdivide a triangular surface mesh — mesh_subdivision
title: Subdivide a triangular surface mesh — mesh_subdivision
text: Applies subdivision scheme triangulated surface mesh using CGAL's polygon mesh processing subdivision algorithms.

title: Subdivide a triangular surface mesh — mesh_subdivision
code: mesh_subdivision(   mesh,   type = c("sqrt3", "loop", "catmull_clark", "doo_sabin"),   n_iterations = 1L,   update_attributes = TRUE )

title: Subdivide a triangular surface mesh — mesh_subdivision
text: mesh mesh3d object. type Subdivision scheme: "sqrt3" (default), "loop", "catmull_clark", "doo_sabin". n_iterations Number subdivision iterations. Default 1L. update_attributes Whether interpolate point cell data input. Default TRUE.

title: Subdivide a triangular surface mesh — mesh_subdivision
text: mesh3d object subdivided surface.

title: Subdivide a triangular surface mesh — mesh_subdivision
code: # \donttest{ f <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa") mesh <- read_stl(f) sub <- mesh_subdivision(mesh, type = "loop", n_iterations = 1L) print(sub) #> <mesh3d> 7200 vertices 14400 faces # }

what: Fill holes in a triangular surface mesh — patch_filling
title: Fill holes in a triangular surface mesh — patch_filling
text: Detects fills holes triangulated surface mesh using CGAL's hole filling algorithm. point_ids supplied, hole whose boundary passes points filled; otherwise detected holes filled.

title: Fill holes in a triangular surface mesh — patch_filling
code: patch_filling(   mesh,   point_ids = integer(0),   fairing_continuity = 1L,   update_attributes = TRUE )

title: Fill holes in a triangular surface mesh — patch_filling
text: mesh mesh3d object. point_ids Integer vector 1-based vertex indices hole boundary fill. Use integer(0) (default) fill holes. fairing_continuity Integer controlling continuity order filled patch: 0 (C0), 1 (C1, default), 2 (C2). update_attributes Whether interpolate point cell data input. Default TRUE.

title: Fill holes in a triangular surface mesh — patch_filling
text: mesh3d object hole(s) filled.

title: Fill holes in a triangular surface mesh — patch_filling
code: f <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa") mesh <- read_stl(f) result <- patch_filling(mesh)  # no-op: torus has no holes

what: Estimate point cloud normals via PCA — pca_estimate_normals
title: Estimate point cloud normals via PCA — pca_estimate_normals
text: Estimates orients surface normals unoriented point cloud using CGAL's PCA-based normal estimation algorithm.

title: Estimate point cloud normals via PCA — pca_estimate_normals
code: pca_estimate_normals(   mesh,   n_neighbors = 18L,   orient = TRUE,   delete_unoriented = TRUE )

title: Estimate point cloud normals via PCA — pca_estimate_normals
text: mesh mesh3d object representing point cloud (may empty $face matrix). n_neighbors Number nearest neighbours used local PCA. Default 18L. orient Whether orient estimated normals consistently. Default TRUE. delete_unoriented Whether remove points whose normals reliably oriented. Default TRUE.

title: Estimate point cloud normals via PCA — pca_estimate_normals
text: mesh3d object identical input additional $normals field (3×N numeric matrix, one column per point).

title: Estimate point cloud normals via PCA — pca_estimate_normals
code: # \donttest{ f     <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa") mesh  <- read_stl(f) cloud <- mesh; cloud$it <- matrix(integer(0), 3L, 0L) cloud_n <- pca_estimate_normals(cloud) # }

what: Reconstruct a surface from an oriented point cloud (Poisson) — poisson_reconstruction
title: Reconstruct a surface from an oriented point cloud (Poisson) — poisson_reconstruction
text: Reconstructs closed triangulated surface oriented point cloud using CGAL's Poisson surface reconstruction algorithm. input point cloud must carry estimated normals (e.g., pca_estimate_normals()).

title: Reconstruct a surface from an oriented point cloud (Poisson) — poisson_reconstruction
code: poisson_reconstruction(   mesh,   min_angle = 20,   max_size = 2,   distance = 0.375,   gen_normals = TRUE )

title: Reconstruct a surface from an oriented point cloud (Poisson) — poisson_reconstruction
text: mesh mesh3d object representing point cloud. Must contain $normals field (3×N matrix) produced pca_estimate_normals(). min_angle Minimum triangle angle output mesh (degrees). Default 20. max_size Maximum triangle size relative average point spacing. Default 2. distance Maximum distance reconstructed surface input points, relative average spacing. Default 0.375. gen_normals Whether generate normals output surface. Default TRUE.

title: Reconstruct a surface from an oriented point cloud (Poisson) — poisson_reconstruction
text: mesh3d object reconstructed surface.

title: Reconstruct a surface from an oriented point cloud (Poisson) — poisson_reconstruction
code: # \donttest{ f     <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa") mesh  <- read_stl(f) cloud <- mesh; cloud$it <- matrix(integer(0), 3L, 0L) cloud_n <- pca_estimate_normals(cloud) result  <- poisson_reconstruction(cloud_n) # }

what: Read a point cloud file — read_points_xyz
title: Read a point cloud file — read_points_xyz
text: Reads point cloud disk using CGAL's point set reader. Supported formats: .las, ., .ply, .xyz.

title: Read a point cloud file — read_points_xyz
code: read_points_xyz(filename)

title: Read a point cloud file — read_points_xyz
text: filename Path point cloud file. file must exist readable.

title: Read a point cloud file — read_points_xyz
text: mesh3d object representing point cloud. $vb 4×N matrix homogeneous point coordinates; $empty 3×0 matrix. file contains normal vectors, returned $normals (3×N matrix).

title: Read a point cloud file — read_points_xyz
code: f <- system.file("testdata", "test_points.xyz", package = "vespa") cloud <- read_points_xyz(f) print(cloud) #> <mesh3d> 50 vertices 0 faces

what: Read a surface mesh from an STL file — read_stl
title: Read a surface mesh from an STL file — read_stl
text: Reads triangulated surface mesh ASCII binary STL file using VTK's STL reader.

title: Read a surface mesh from an STL file — read_stl
code: read_stl(filename)

title: Read a surface mesh from an STL file — read_stl
text: filename Path STL file. file must exist readable.

title: Read a surface mesh from an STL file — read_stl
text: mesh3d object. $vb 4×N matrix homogeneous vertex coordinates; $3×M matrix 1-based triangle indices.

title: Read a surface mesh from an STL file — read_stl
code: f <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa") mesh <- read_stl(f) print(mesh) #> <mesh3d> 1800 vertices 3600 faces

what: Read a surface mesh or point cloud from a VTP file — read_vtp
title: Read a surface mesh or point cloud from a VTP file — read_vtp
text: Reads vtkPolyData object VTK XML PolyData (.vtp) file using VTK's XML reader. ASCII binary (including binary-appended) formats supported.

title: Read a surface mesh or point cloud from a VTP file — read_vtp
code: read_vtp(filename)

title: Read a surface mesh or point cloud from a VTP file — read_vtp
text: filename Path .vtp file. file must exist readable.

title: Read a surface mesh or point cloud from a VTP file — read_vtp
text: mesh3d object. $vb 4×N matrix homogeneous vertex coordinates; $3×M matrix 1-based triangle indices. point-cloud VTP files (polygon cells), $3×0 empty matrix.

title: Read a surface mesh or point cloud from a VTP file — read_vtp
code: # \donttest{ url <- paste0(   "https://gitlab.kitware.com/vtk/meshing/vespa/-/raw/master/",   "Data/Testing/hand.vtp?ref_type=heads" ) tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".vtp") download.file(url, tmp, quiet = TRUE) mesh <- read_vtp(tmp) #> Error: XMLPolyDataReader: ERROR: In ./IO/XML/vtkXMLReader.cxx, line 521 #> vtkXMLPolyDataReader (0x55aa576e4bd0): Error parsing input file.  ReadXMLInformation aborting. #>  print(mesh) #> Error: object 'mesh' not found # }

what: Fair a region of a triangular surface mesh — region_fairing
title: Fair a region of a triangular surface mesh — region_fairing
text: Smooths user-defined region triangulated surface mesh using CGAL's region fairing algorithm. region vertices moved minimise local fairing energy boundary region kept fixed.

title: Fair a region of a triangular surface mesh — region_fairing
code: region_fairing(mesh, point_ids, update_attributes = TRUE)

title: Fair a region of a triangular surface mesh — region_fairing
text: mesh mesh3d object. point_ids Integer vector 1-based vertex indices defining region faired. update_attributes Whether interpolate point cell data input. Default TRUE.

title: Fair a region of a triangular surface mesh — region_fairing
text: mesh3d object selected region faired.

title: Fair a region of a triangular surface mesh — region_fairing
code: f <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa") mesh <- read_stl(f) result <- region_fairing(mesh, point_ids = 1:5)

title: SDF volume objects — sdf_volume
text: sdf_volume 3-D scalar field defined regular grid. typically produced signed_distance_function(), negative values lie inside source mesh positive values outside.

title: SDF volume objects — sdf_volume
text: dims Integer vector length 3: grid dimensions (nx, ny, nz). spacing Numeric vector length 3: voxel size along axis. origin Numeric vector length 3: world-space origin grid. array 3-D numeric array signed distances (x fastest).

what: Shape-preserving smoothing of a triangular surface mesh — shape_smoothing
title: Shape-preserving smoothing of a triangular surface mesh — shape_smoothing
text: Smooths triangulated surface preserving overall shape, using CGAL's mean curvature flow algorithm.

title: Shape-preserving smoothing of a triangular surface mesh — shape_smoothing
code: shape_smoothing(   mesh,   n_iterations = 1L,   time_step = 1e-04,   update_attributes = TRUE )

title: Shape-preserving smoothing of a triangular surface mesh — shape_smoothing
text: mesh mesh3d object. n_iterations Number smoothing iterations. Default 1L. time_step Controls smoothing speed. Smaller values give conservative smoothing. Default 1e-4. update_attributes Whether interpolate point cell data input. Default TRUE.

title: Shape-preserving smoothing of a triangular surface mesh — shape_smoothing
text: mesh3d object smoothed surface.

title: Shape-preserving smoothing of a triangular surface mesh — shape_smoothing
code: # \donttest{ f <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa") mesh <- read_stl(f) result <- shape_smoothing(mesh, n_iterations = 2L) # }

what: Compute a signed distance function (SDF) volume from a surface mesh — signed_distance_function
title: Compute a signed distance function (SDF) volume from a surface mesh — signed_distance_function
text: Generates 3-D signed distance field triangulated surface mesh using CGAL's signed distance function. Negative values inside mesh, positive values outside.

title: Compute a signed distance function (SDF) volume from a surface mesh — signed_distance_function
code: signed_distance_function(mesh, base_resolution = 64L, padding = 0L)

title: Compute a signed distance function (SDF) volume from a surface mesh — signed_distance_function
text: mesh mesh3d object. mesh closed non-self-intersecting physically meaningful signed distances. base_resolution Number grid nodes along shortest bounding-box axis. Default 64L. padding Number voxels padding added side axis around mesh bounding box. Default 0L.

title: Compute a signed distance function (SDF) volume from a surface mesh — signed_distance_function
text: object class sdf_volume (list subclass) elements: dims Integer vector length 3: grid dimensions (nx, ny, nz). spacing Numeric vector length 3: voxel size direction. origin Numeric vector length 3: world-space origin grid. array 3-D numeric array shape dims (x fastest, VTK/Fortran order) containing signed distances. Use .vector(sdf$array) recover flat vector. object accepted extract_isosurface() dedicated print() plot() methods.

title: Compute a signed distance function (SDF) volume from a surface mesh — signed_distance_function
text: Grid resolution cubic voxels. Let \(L_{\min} = \min(L_x, L_y, L_z)\) shortest bounding-box extent. voxel size $$h = \frac{L_{\min}}{b - 1},$$ \(b\) base_resolution. number grid nodes along axis $$N_{\alpha} = \Bigl\lfloor \frac{L_{\alpha}}{h} \Bigr\rceil + 1 + 2\,p,$$ \(\lfloor\cdot\rceil\) denotes rounding nearest integer \(p\) padding. guarantees voxels cubic regardless mesh aspect ratio.

title: Compute a signed distance function (SDF) volume from a surface mesh — signed_distance_function
code: # \donttest{ f <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa") mesh <- read_stl(f) sdf  <- signed_distance_function(mesh, base_resolution = 32L) #> SDF grid: 135 × 135 × 32 — 583,200 voxels, ~4.4 Mb sdf                              # uses print.sdf_volume #> <sdf_volume> #>   dims:    135 x 135 x 32  (583,200 voxels) #>   spacing: 0.0194 x 0.0193 x 0.01935 #>   origin:  (-1.3, -1.293, -0.3) #>   values:  [-0.2899, 0.7404] range(sdf$array)                 # negative inside, positive outside #> [1] -0.2899068  0.7403798 # }

what: vespa: R Bindings for the Vespa VTK/CGAL Mesh Processing Library. — vespa-package
title: vespa: R Bindings for the Vespa VTK/CGAL Mesh Processing Library. — vespa-package
text: Provides R wrappers Vespa filters (isotropic remeshing, boolean operations, alpha wrapping, surface reconstruction, point set processing, ) via direct Rcpp bindings Vespa C++/VTK library.

title: vespa: R Bindings for the Vespa VTK/CGAL Mesh Processing Library. — vespa-package
text: Maintainer: Christophe Regouby christophe.regouby@airbus.com Authors: Christophe Regouby christophe.regouby@airbus.com

title: vespa 0.4.0
text: Add extract_isosurface() sdf_volume objects. signed_distance_function() now return sdf_volume object. Add Signed Distance Function Poisson Surface Reconstruction vignettes.

title: vespa 0.3.0
text: Renaming exported functions better transparency.

title: vespa 0.2.0
text: Bump vespa version 1.0.0.

title: vespa 0.1.0
text: Initial vespa wrapper.
